install_tell: pip install hayhooks && hayhooks run   (Docker: docker compose up from deepset-ai/hayhooks compose.yml, which runs `hayhooks run --host 0.0.0.0`)
misconfig_patterns: Exposing the documented compose.yml port mapping 1416:1416 directly to the internet (binds 0.0.0.0, no reverse-proxy auth)
HAYHOOKS_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS=["*"] left at compose default -> any web origin can call the API from a victim browser
HAYHOOKS_SHOW_TRACEBACKS=true (compose default) in production -> secret/stack-trace disclosure
Open WebUI front-end run with WEBUI_AUTH=false (deepset compose default) exposed publicly
Relying on the pip default host=localhost assumption while actually deploying via the Docker image, which overrides to 0.0.0.0

active_probe.false_positive_check: `{"status":"Up!"}` alone is generic (many health endpoints return Up/OK). Disambiguate by requiring the Hayhooks-unique pairing: the `/status` JSON must contain BOTH the `"status"` string AND a top-level `"pipelines"` array, OR fetch `/openapi.json` and confirm the vendor-unique operationIds `status_all`, `status_pipeline`, `pipeline_deploy`, `yaml_pipeline_deploy`, `draw` and the `/deploy_files` + `/deploy-yaml` paths. Port 1416/1417 + FastAPI `/docs` is corroborating but not sufficient alone (1416 is not registered/common, but any FastAPI app can answer /docs). Do not match on the word "pipeline" or "chat" generically.

deployment_tells: Listening on TCP 1416 (REST) and 1417 (MCP) - 1416 is a vendor-distinctive port pair
Container CMD `hayhooks run --host 0.0.0.0`; uvicorn banner 'Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:1416'
Often co-deployed with Open WebUI on host port 3000 (WEBUI_AUTH=false) pointed at OPENAI_API_BASE_URL=http://hayhooks:1416
pivot_paths: GET /status -> enumerate deployed pipeline names; then GET /status/{name} and GET /draw/{name} to read each pipeline's component graph / business logic (read-only)
GET /openapi.json + /docs -> map every deployed /{pipeline}/run route and its expected inputs without prior knowledge
GET /{pipeline}/run or POST /v1/chat/completions -> exercise the RAG pipeline to surface ingested-document content and the backing vector store via normal query path (read-only)
Trigger an error (malformed run body) -> with HAYHOOKS_SHOW_TRACEBACKS=true read stack traces that can leak env paths and secrets (read-only observation)
If co-located Open WebUI on :3000 with WEBUI_AUTH=false -> unauth admin of the chat UI and its model/connection config (separate component, read-only enumeration)
vulnerabilities: No CVE/GHSA published for Hayhooks as of 2026-06-12 (deepset-ai/hayhooks security advisories page: 'There aren't any published security advisories').

Design-level unauth RCE class (no CVE): POST /deploy_files and POST /deploy-yaml accept and load arbitrary pipeline_wrapper.py Python / YAML over HTTP with no authentication in any shipped version; affected: all versions (no auth feature exists in settings.py).

Design-level info-leak: compose default HAYHOOKS_SHOW_TRACEBACKS=true returns full stack traces (potential secret/path disclosure) to unauthenticated callers; affected: all versions when run via the official compose.yml.
auth_evidence: Primary source: deepset-ai/hayhooks `compose.yml` (the documented Docker quickstart) runs `command: sh -c "pip install --quiet trafilatura && hayhooks run --host 0.0.0.0"`, publishes `ports: ['1416:1416','1417:1417']`, sets `HAYHOOKS_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS=["*"]` and `HAYHOOKS_SHOW_TRACEBACKS=true`, and defines NO auth variable. `docker/Dockerfile` hard-codes `CMD ["hayhooks","run","--host","0.0.0.0"]` and `EXPOSE 1416`. `src/hayhooks/settings.py` has no auth/password/token/api_key field at all — only `disable_ssl: bool = False` and `cors_allow_origins: list[str] = ["*"]` (which the code logs a warning about). The companion deepset-ai/hayhooks-open-webui-docker-compose sets `WEBUI_AUTH=false` on the Open WebUI front-end. The deepset docker quickstart docs tell users the Open WebUI connection field is `"API Key": "any-value" (not used by Hayhooks)`. The REST routers (status.py, deploy.py) carry NO FastAPI auth dependency: `GET /status` and `POST /deploy_files` (body field `files` incl. `pipeline_wrapper.py`) / `POST /deploy-yaml` (body field `source_code`) all answer unauthenticated. Note: the bare pip/CLI default binds `host="localhost"` (loopback), but every documented internet-facing path (Docker image + compose) binds 0.0.0.0 with zero auth.
sensitive_assets: Deployed Haystack pipeline/agent definitions (business logic: retrievers, prompt templates, routing, system prompts) readable via /status/{name} and /draw/{name}

Upstream LLM provider API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY and other provider keys) injected as env and used by deployed pipelines; HAYHOOKS_SHOW_TRACEBACKS=true (compose default) leaks secrets/stack traces in error responses
Ingested RAG corpus / document store contents and vector store reachable through the pipeline's own /{pipeline}/run query path (and any fronting Weaviate/Qdrant/Elasticsearch the pipeline points at)
Conversation/chat history via the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions backend

Arbitrary code execution surface: POST /deploy_files (files['pipeline_wrapper.py'] Python) and POST /deploy-yaml (source_code) deploy and load user-supplied Python/YAML at runtime with no auth, yielding RCE in the container and pivot to its env/secrets
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
